Full job description
Role
This is an exciting opportunity to work with our prestigious client as an Data quality & IT Manager.

Working days and hours are Monday - Friday, 37 hours a week.

Supporting the practice to ensure all the QOF IIF and any other targets are being met using the Ardens and bespoke searches to monitor progress. Creating reports based on these searches to keep the practice informed of progress towards targets. Interrogating EMIS data to ensure the templates are being completed correctly to maximise income. Taking responsibility for all IT systems and ensuring staff are able to use all the practice systems effectively.

Duties and responsibilities
Information technology

Ensure the practice IT systems are kept up to date and implement necessary changes
Keep up to date with new developments to implement which could benefit the practice
Maintain a log of all practice IT equipment
Deal with IT problems
Data Management

Monitor and keep track of all practice targets (QOF, IIF, QIF etc) reporting back to practice manager where targets are not being met.
Run searches and monitor templates to ensure good data quality, reporting issues to relevant team leader and Practice Manager
Run and devise searches on request of practice staff
Review of patient records to ensure the data is correct and up to date
Review of Ardens searches and ensure they are providing the correct information
Support the Care Coordinator to run searches and recall of patients in long term conditions clinics
Monitor of all practice targets and submit claims and declare achievements
Confidentiality:

In the course of seeking treatment, patients entrust us with, or allow us to gather, sensitive information in relation to their health and other matters. They do so in confidence and have the right to expect that staff will respect their privacy and act appropriately
In the performance of the duties outlined in this job description, the post-holder may have access to confidential information relating to patients and their carers, practice staff and other healthcare workers. They may also have access to information relating to the practice as a business organisation. All such information from any source is to be regarded as strictly confidential
Information relating to patients, carers, colleagues, other healthcare workers or the business of the practice may only be divulged to authorised persons in accordance with the practice policies and procedures relating to confidentiality and the protection of personal and sensitive data
Health & safety:

The post-holder will implement and lead on the full range of promotion and management of their own and others’ health, safety and security as defined in the practice health & safety policy, the practice health & safety manual, and the practice infection control policy and published procedures. This will include (but will not be limited to):

Ensuring job holders across the practice adhere to their individual responsibilities for infection control and health and safety, using a system of observation, audit and check, hazard identification, questioning, reporting and risk management.
Maintaining an up-to-date knowledge of health and safety and infection control (statutory and best practice guidelines) and ensure implementation across the business
Using personal security systems within the workplace, according to practice guidelines
Identifying the risks involved in work activities and undertaking such activities in a way that manages those risks across the business
Making effective use of training to update knowledge and skills, and initiate and manage the training of others
Using appropriate infection control procedures, maintaining work areas in a tidy and safe way and free from hazards, and initiating remedial / corrective action where needed
Actively identifying, reporting, and correcting health and safety hazards and infection hazards immediately when recognised
Keeping own work areas and general / patient areas generally clean, identifying issues and hazards / risks in relation to other work areas within the business, and assuming responsibility in the maintenance of general standards of cleanliness across the business in consultation (where appropriate) with other sector managers
Undertaking periodic infection control training (minimum annually)
Routine management of own team / team areas, and maintenance of work space standards
Demonstrate due regard for safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children.
Equality and Diversity:

The post-holder will support the equality, diversity and rights of patients, carers and colleagues, to include:

Acting in a way that recognises the importance of people’s rights, interpreting them in a way that is consistent with practice procedures and policies, and current legislation
Respecting the privacy, dignity, needs and beliefs of patients, carers and colleagues
Behaving in a manner that is welcoming to and of the individual, is non-judgmental and respects their circumstances, feelings priorities and rights.
Personal/professional development:

The post-holder will participate in any training programme implemented by the practice as part of this employment, such training to include:

Participation in an annual individual performance review, including taking responsibility for maintaining a record of own personal and/or professional development
Taking responsibility for own development, learning and performance and demonstrating skills and activities to others who are undertaking similar work
Quality:

The post-holder will strive to maintain quality within the practice, and will:

Alert other team members to issues of quality and risk
Assess own performance and take accountability for own actions, either directly or under supervision
Contribute to the effectiveness of the team by reflecting on own and team activities and making suggestions on ways to improve and enhance the team’s performance
Work effectively with individuals in other agencies to meet patients’ needs
Effectively manage own time, workload and resources
Contribution to the implementation of services:

Apply practice policies, standards and guidance
Discuss with other team members how policies, standards and guidelines will affect own work
Participate in audit where appropriate
Skills and experience

Communicate effectively with other team members
Communicate effectively with patients and carers
Recognise people’s needs for alternative methods of communication and respond accordingly
Pay: £34,592.00 per year

Work Location: In person

shortRoleReason: Role title is clearly stated at the top of the advert.
companySummary: Unitemps is advertising on behalf of a prestigious client for a practice-based data quality and IT management role.
roleSummary: The role combines practice IT support with data quality and target monitoring, including searches, reporting, EMIS data review, and support for QOF/IIF-related activity.
headlineAttraction: Combine IT management with data quality work in a practice setting.
rolePurpose: To manage practice IT systems and data quality processes so the practice can monitor performance, meet targets, and maximise income.
probablePriorities: Keep staff able to use systems effectively
advertSummary: A full-time, Monday to Friday, in-person Data Quality & IT Manager role in Stone paying £34,592 a year. The post focuses on maintaining practice IT systems, improving data quality, running searches and reports, and supporting achievement of practice targets.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits right where I do my best work: keeping systems reliable, improving the quality of the data behind decisions, and making sure people can use the tools properly. In my current NHS primary care role I’ve already worked across reporting, performance control and patient-facing service improvement, so the mix here feels practical and familiar.
whyThisRole: This role fits me because it combines IT support with the discipline of monitoring targets, checking data and making sure reports are accurate enough to act on. I like work where the standard is clear: systems need to work, searches need to be right, and the practice needs reliable information to support QOF, IIF and income protection.

Create reports on progress towards targets
Support the Care Coordinator with searches and patient recalls

routine management of own team / team areas
likelyBusinessNeeds: Maximise income through correct template completion
Maintain secure and effective IT systems
impliedStrategicGoals: Strengthen data quality across the practice
Support service delivery through better system use

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can keep practice systems stable, make data trustworthy, and turn searches and reports into something the team can actually use. It also needs calm ownership: spotting issues early, protecting confidentiality, and keeping the practice informed when targets are off track.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Maintain and improve practice IT systems
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led day-to-day operations in environments where process reliability and quick problem-solving mattered, and I’m used to keeping systems working rather than just reacting when they fail.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role needs someone who will own the basics properly and keep disruption down.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Run searches and monitor data quality
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked with sensitive, business-critical information and managed structured workloads where accuracy and control were essential.
experienceMappings.relevance: That translates well to search quality, record checking and careful reporting.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Support QOF, IIF and target monitoring
experienceMappings.myEvidence: In NHS primary care I’ve already worked with performance reporting and service improvement, so I understand the value of clear measures and follow-through.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role depends on turning data into action, not just producing reports.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Communicate with staff, patients and carers
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve improved patient-facing outcomes in a live primary care setting, which means I’m comfortable balancing operational detail with the human side of service delivery.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role sits between systems and people, so communication matters as much as technical accuracy.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve coordinated complex annual work while maintaining service standards and supporting a team, so I’m used to keeping several moving parts under control.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is important in a role with IT issues, data checks and target monitoring all running together.

focusAreasToBring.title: Reliable system ownership

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring NHS primary care experience, a strong reporting mindset and a track record of improving how operations run in regulated environments. I’m comfortable with the mix of detail, confidentiality and practical problem-solving that this role needs, and I’ve already worked with EMIS, Power BI and patient-facing service improvement.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months getting a clear grip on the practice’s systems, target reporting and data quality issues. I’d want to understand how searches, templates and claims currently work, then help make the reporting more reliable and the day-to-day use of systems easier for staff.
companyHighlights: Unitemps is recruiting on behalf of a prestigious client.
The role is based in Stone and is fully in person.
The post is Monday to Friday, 37 hours a week.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best where people value accuracy, confidentiality and steady follow-through. I’m comfortable in a setting where I need to be dependable, practical and easy to work with.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would learn how the practice uses EMIS, Ardens, searches and reports, and get a clear view of the current IT setup, data quality issues and target position.
I would start checking templates, search outputs and reporting routines more closely, while building a clearer picture of recurring issues and quick wins.
I would aim to make target monitoring, issue reporting and routine system support more consistent, so the practice has better control over performance and claims.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring calm ownership, accurate reporting and practical NHS experience to a role that depends on getting the detail right.
closingProofPoints: I improved patient experience in NHS primary care.
I’ve worked in a regulated, data-sensitive environment.
I’ve built reporting and performance control across complex operations.
contentNotes: I have kept company-specific claims limited because the advert gives little beyond Unitemps acting for a prestigious client.
I have used only evidence-bank examples and avoided inventing direct EMIS/QOF delivery claims beyond what the CV supports.
The strongest fit is NHS primary care plus regulated data handling, so I have weighted those examples most heavily.
